quiz.title: Unit 9 Quiz 1, Biology I
quiz.subtitle: Genetics: Alleles, Genotypes, and Mendelian Inheritance
quiz.subject: Biology
quiz.topic: Alleles, Genetic Locus, Homozygous vs Heterozygous, Dominant vs Recessive, Genotype vs Phenotype, Chromosomes, Mendel's Principles

questions.question: Alleles are:
options: Different genes on the same chromosome
Alternative forms of the same gene
questions.explanation: Alleles are alternative versions of the same gene that occupy the same locus on homologous chromosomes. They can be dominant or recessive and result in different traits (e.g., brown vs blue eyes).

questions.question: Which of the following statements accurately describes the biological relationship between genetic components and their physical expression?
options: Genes and alleles represent identical biological concepts and can be used interchangeably.
Alleles act as the specific versions of a gene that produce different variations of a trait.
Dominant alleles possess a superior physical strength that directly destroys recessive genes.
Recessive alleles permanently vanish from the genetic code when paired with a dominant one.
questions.explanation: Alleles are different versions of the same gene that produce variations in traits (e.g., blue vs brown eye color). Genes and alleles are not identical concepts—genes are DNA segments, alleles are variants. Dominant alleles do not destroy recessive alleles; recessive alleles remain present in the genotype even if not expressed in the phenotype.

questions.question: Which description provides the most accurate biological definition of a genetic locus?
options: The specific physiological role a gene performs.
The physical location of a gene on a chromosome.
The particular molecular variant of a single gene.
The permanent alteration of a genetic sequence.
questions.explanation: A genetic locus is the specific physical location of a gene on a chromosome. The physiological role describes gene function, variants are alleles, and permanent alterations are mutations.

questions.question: An organism with two identical alleles for a trait is said to be:
questions.explanation: Homozygous means having two identical alleles for a trait (e.g., AA or aa). Heterozygous means having different alleles (Aa). Dominant/recessive describe allele expression, not zygosity.

questions.question: An organism with two different alleles for a trait is:
questions.explanation: Heterozygous means having two different alleles for a trait (e.g., Aa). Homozygous means identical alleles, haploid refers to one set of chromosomes, diploid means two sets.

questions.question: Which description accurately characterizes the biological behavior of a dominant allele within an organism's genetic makeup?
options: It represents the version of a gene that appears most frequently in a given population.
It expresses its particular trait by concealing the presence of a different gene variant.
It requires the presence of two identical copies to manifest a specific physical feature.
It acts as a primary catalyst for introducing permanent changes into the DNA sequence.
questions.explanation: A dominant allele expresses its trait even when paired with a recessive allele, effectively masking the recessive allele's expression. Frequency in population is not related to dominance.

questions.question: Under which specific genetic condition does a recessive trait successfully manifest its physical characteristics?
options: When it possesses the strength to override a different gene version.
When it exists alongside a dominant variant of the same gene.
When it appears in a matching pair without a dominant partner.
When it undergoes a spontaneous change in its genetic sequence.
questions.explanation: A recessive trait is expressed only when the organism is homozygous recessive (aa) - meaning both alleles are recessive with no dominant allele present to mask them.

questions.question: Which term refers to the complete internal set of genetic instructions inherited by a living organism?
questions.explanation: Genotype is the genetic makeup (allele combination) of an organism. Phenotype is the observable expression, trait is a specific characteristic, and character is a feature like eye color.

questions.question: The observable characteristics of an organism are its:
questions.explanation: Phenotype refers to the physical expression of traits (what you see). Genotype is the genetic code, alleles are gene versions, and locus is the gene position.

questions.question: Which genotype represents a heterozygous condition?
questions.explanation: Aa represents heterozygous (two different alleles). AA is homozygous dominant, aa is homozygous recessive, and A alone is not a valid diploid genotype.

questions.question: Which genotype would express a recessive trait?
questions.explanation: Recessive traits are expressed only in homozygous recessive genotype (aa). AA expresses dominant, Aa expresses dominant (heterozygous), and A– is incomplete notation.

questions.question: Genes are located on:
questions.explanation: Genes are segments of DNA located on chromosomes within the nucleus. Ribosomes synthesize proteins, proteins are gene products, and cell membranes are cellular boundaries.

questions.question: Humans normally have how many alleles for each gene?
questions.explanation: Humans are diploid organisms with two sets of chromosomes, so they have two alleles for each gene—one inherited from each parent (except for genes on sex chromosomes in males).

questions.question: The inheritance of alleles follows principles first described by:
questions.explanation: Gregor Mendel discovered the fundamental principles of inheritance through pea plant experiments in the 1860s. Darwin proposed evolution, Watson and Crick discovered DNA structure.

questions.question: If an individual has the genotype Aa, the expressed trait will be:
questions.explanation: In complete dominance, the dominant allele (A) masks the recessive allele (a), so the expressed trait is dominant. Blending occurs in incomplete dominance, not here.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered the fundamentals of genetics! 🧬
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review the differences between homozygous and heterozygous.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on dominant vs recessive allele expression.
scoreMessages.0: Please review allele definitions, genotype vs phenotype, and Mendel's principles.
